prompt:
Create a function called 'create_forum_post' that generates an HTML representation of a user's post for a public forum. The function should display the post's title, author, content, and tags, with the tags shown as separate elements. The function's name should be create_forum_post. The function should take 4 arguments, i.e., - poster: str, the author of the post, - heading: str, the title of the user's post, - body: str, the main content of the user's post, - labels: list(str), a list of tags associated with the post. The function should return returns a string containing the sanitized HTML representation of the user's post. The following setup code will be provided to you: ``` USER_POST_FORMAT = "

{heading}

Posted by {poster}

{body}
{labels_html}
" ``` Here's some additional context about the imported packages and global variables: USER_POST_FORMAT: str, a predefined HTML template string with placeholders for the post's components (poster, heading, body, and labels) Please implement the function according to the description and setup above. Output the code in a markdown code block, i.e., between triple backticks (```) with the language specified as Python. EXAMPLE INPUT/OUTPUT: { "input": { "poster": "JohnDoe", "heading": "My First Post", "body": "Hello, this is my first post!", "labels": [ "Introduction", "FirstPost" ] }, "output": "

My First Post

Posted by JohnDoe

Hello, this is my first post!
Introduction FirstPost
" }
code:
USER_POST_FORMAT = "

{heading}

Posted by {poster}

{body}
{labels_html}
" def create_forum_post(poster: str, heading: str, body: str, labels: list[str]) -> str: """ Generates an HTML representation of a user's post for a public forum. The function displays the post's title, author, content, and tags, with the tags shown as separate elements. Args: poster: The author of the post. heading: The title of the user's post. body: The main content of the user's post. labels: A list of tags associated with the post. Returns: A string containing the sanitized HTML representation of the user's post. """ labels_html = " ".join([f"{label}" for label in labels]) return USER_POST_FORMAT.format( heading=heading, poster=poster, body=body, labels_html=labels_html ) if __name__ == '__main__': # Example Usage: post_details = { "poster": "JohnDoe", "heading": "My First Post", "body": "Hello, this is my first post!", "labels": [ "Introduction", "FirstPost" ] } html_output = create_forum_post( poster=post_details["poster"], heading=post_details["heading"], body=post_details["body"], labels=post_details["labels"] ) expected_output = "

My First Post

Posted by JohnDoe

Hello, this is my first post!
Introduction FirstPost
" print(f"Generated HTML: {html_output}") assert html_output == expected_output print("Assertion passed: Generated HTML matches expected output.") post_details_2 = { "poster": "JaneSmith", "heading": "Python Tips", "body": "Here are some useful Python tips.", "labels": ["Python", "Programming", "Tips"] } html_output_2 = create_forum_post(**post_details_2) expected_output_2 = "

Python Tips

Posted by JaneSmith

Here are some useful Python tips.
Python Programming Tips
" print(f"Generated HTML 2: {html_output_2}") assert html_output_2 == expected_output_2 print("Assertion passed: Generated HTML 2 matches expected output.")
